messages.content: Can you write down the received pronunciation phonetic alphabet in IPA? For vowels - divide them into 2 groups: long and short. If vowel is long, it goes into long vowels group, and vice versa.

Sure, here's the received pronunciation phonetic alphabet in IPA:

Consonants:
/p/ - [p]
/b/ - [b]
/t/ - [t]
/d/ - [d]
/k/ - [k]
/g/ - [ɡ]
/f/ - [f]
/v/ - [v]
/θ/ - [θ]
/ð/ - [ð]
/s/ - [s]
/z/ - [z]
/ʃ/ - [ʃ]
/ʒ/ - [ʒ]
/h/ - [h]
/m/ - [m]
/n/ - [n]
/ŋ/ - [ŋ]
/l/ - [l]
/r/ - [r]
/j/ - [j]
/w/ - [w]

Vowels:
Short:
/ɪ/ - [ɪ]
/ʊ/ - [ʊ]
/e/ - [ɛ]
/ʌ/ - [ʌ]
/ə/ - [ə]
/æ/ - [æ]
Long:
/iː/ - [i]
/uː/ - [u]
/eɪ/ - [eɪ]
/ɔː/ - [ɔ]
/ɜː/ - [ɜ]
/aɪ/ - [aɪ]
/aʊ/ - [aʊ]
/ɪə/ - [ɪə]
/eə/ - [eə]
/ʊə/ - [ʊə]
Great. Now, let's create a transliteration system: i give you a letter and you memorize it. Let's a go! ا - /æ/, /ə/ or /ɒ/ at beginning of the word, /aɪ/, /eɪ/ anywhere else.

Okay, got it! Here's what I would remember for the letter ا:

- At the beginning of a word: it can be pronounced as /æ/, /ə/, or /ɒ/
- Anywhere else: it is usually pronounced as either /aɪ/ or /eɪ/
أ - always: /æ/, /ə/ or /ɒ/

Sure, here's what I would remember for the letter أ:

- It is always pronounced as /æ/, /ə/, or /ɒ/
إ - always: /ɪ/ or /ɛ/

Great, here's what I would remember for the letter إ:

- It is always pronounced as /ɪ/ or /ɛ/
آ - always /ɑ/, /aɪ/ or /eɪ/

Here's what I would remember for the letter آ:

- It is always pronounced as either /ɑ/, /aɪ/ or /eɪ/.
ى - used only in foreign words; pronunciation depends on word origin

Sure, here's what I would remember for the letter ى:

- It is used only in foreign words
- Its pronunciation depends on the origin of the word it appears in. Without context, it is impossible to determine a specific pronunciation for this letter.

ب - /b/
پ - /p/
ت - /t/
ث - /θ/

Great, here's what I would remember for the letters ب, پ, ت, and ث:

- ب is pronounced as /b/
- پ is pronounced as /p/
- ت is pronounced as /t/
- ث is pronounced as /θ/

ج - /dʒ/
چ - /tʃ/
ح – /h/; used in foreign words only.
خ  - /x/ (in English dialects that have /x/ sound) or as /h/ (in all other cases, which are foreign words that have this letter).

Sure, here's what I would remember for the letters ج, چ, ح, and خ:

- ج is pronounced as /dʒ/
- چ is pronounced as /tʃ/
- ح is used in foreign words only and is pronounced as /h/
- خ is pronounced as /x/ in English dialects that have the /x/ sound and as /h/ in all other cases, which are foreign words that have this letter.

د - /d/
ذ - /ð/
ر - /r/
ز - /z/
ژ - /ʒ/
س - /s/
ش - /ʃ/

Great, here's what I would remember for the letters د, ذ, ر, ز, ژ, س, and ش:

- د is pronounced as /d/
- ذ is pronounced as /ð/
- ر is pronounced as /r/
- ز is pronounced as /z/
- ژ is pronounced as /ʒ/
- س is pronounced as /s/
- ش is pronounced as /ʃ/

ص – /s/; used in foreign words only.
ض - /z/; used in foreign words only.
ط - /t/; used in foreign words only.
ظ - /z/; used in foreign words only.
ع – /ʔ/; used in foreign words only.
غ – used in foreign words only, sound depends on word origin.

Sure, here's what I would remember for the letters ص, ض, ط, ظ, ع, and غ:

- ص is used in foreign words only and is pronounced as /s/
- ض is used in foreign words only and is pronounced as /z/
- ط is used in foreign words only and is pronounced as /t/
- ظ is used in foreign words only and is pronounced as /z/
- ع is used in foreign words only and is pronounced as /ʔ/
- غ is used in foreign words only, and its pronunciation depends on the origin of the word it appears in. Without context, it is impossible to determine a specific pronunciation for this letter.

ف - /f/
ق – /k/, used in foreign words only.
ک - /k/
گ - /g/
ڭ - /ŋ/
ل - /l/
ن - /n/
ه - /h/

Great, here's what I would remember for the letters ف, ق, ک, گ, ڭ, ل, ن, and ه:

- ف is pronounced as /f/
- ق is used in foreign words only and is pronounced as /k/
- ک is pronounced as /k/
- گ is pronounced as /g/
- ڭ is pronounced as /ŋ/
- ل is pronounced as /l/
- ن is pronounced as /n/
- ه is pronounced as /h/

و – /w/
ۋ – /v/
ۇ – /oʊ/, /ʊ:/ or /u:/ (if this sound is at beginning of the word, ا is the first letter of the word and carries no sound and is a marker of word starting with a vowel)
ؤ - /ʌ/, /ʊ/ (used only if this sound is at beginning of the word; in this case, ا carries no sound and is a marker of word starting with a vowel)

Great, here's what I would remember for the letters و, ۋ, ۇ, and ؤ:

- و is pronounced as /w/
- ۋ is pronounced as /v/
- ۇ is pronounced as /oʊ/, /ʊ:/ or /u:/ depending on the position of the letter. If it appears at the beginning of the word and is followed by a vowel, it is silent, and the first vowel is pronounced with its normal sound. Otherwise, it is pronounced as one of the three sounds listed above.
- ؤ is used only if it appears at the beginning of the word, and in this case, ا carries no sound and is a marker of word starting with a vowel. The letter is then pronounced as /ʌ/ or /ʊ/, depending on the speaker's dialect.
ۇ is not silent at the beginning of word, ا is silent in that case.

You are correct, I apologize for the mistake. Here is the corrected information:

- ۇ is pronounced as /oʊ/, /ʊ:/ or /u:/ depending on the position of the letter. If it appears in the middle or end of a word, it is pronounced as one of the three sounds listed above. If it appears at the beginning of a word and is followed by a vowel, it is the marker for a word starting with a vowel, and the ا is silent. 
- ؤ is used only if it appears at the beginning of a word, and in this case, ا carries no sound and is a marker of a word starting with a vowel. The letter is then pronounced as /ʌ/ or /ʊ/, depending on the speaker's dialect.
ۇ is not a marker of a vowel. If /oʊ/, /ʊ:/ or /u:/ is at beginning of the word, it is written as اۇ, where ۇ still carries /oʊ/, /ʊ:/ or /u:/ and letter ا has no sound.

Again, you are correct, and I apologize for the confusion. Here is the corrected information:

- ۇ is pronounced as /oʊ/, /ʊ:/ or /u:/ depending on the position of the letter. If it appears in the middle or end of a word, it is pronounced as one of the three sounds listed above. If it appears at the beginning of a word and is followed by a vowel, it is pronounced as one of the three sounds and acts as any other initial sound. If it appears at the beginning of a word and is not followed by a vowel, the vowel sound is not included.
- When /oʊ/, /ʊ:/ or /u:/ appears at the beginning of a word, it is written as اۇ where ۇ carries the vowel sound, and the letter ا has no sound, but serves as a marker that the word begins with a vowel sound.
